questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Gol Gumbaz located?
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz stands in Bijapur (Vijayapura), which lies in the state of Karnataka.

questions.q: What type of structure is the Gol Gumbaz?
questions.explanation: The Gol Gumbaz is a mausoleum, built as the tomb of Sultan Mohammed Adil Shah.

questions.q: Whose tomb is the Gol Gumbaz?
questions.explanation: The Gol Gumbaz was built as the tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah, the seventh ruler of the Adil Shahi dynasty of Bijapur.

questions.q: The Gol Gumbaz is most famous for being crowned by what feature?
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz is renowned for its huge hemispherical dome, one of the largest in the world.

questions.q: The acoustic feature inside the Gol Gumbaz where even a whisper carries across the dome is known as the what?
questions.explanation: The circular gallery beneath the dome is famous as the Whispering Gallery, where whispers can be heard across its width.

questions.q: In which city is the Gol Gumbaz situated?
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz is located in the city of Bijapur, officially renamed Vijayapura, in Karnataka.

questions.q: In which century was the Gol Gumbaz built?
questions.explanation: The Gol Gumbaz was constructed in the 17th century, completed around 1656.

questions.q: The name 'Gol Gumbaz' broadly refers to what?
questions.explanation: The name derives from terms meaning a round or circular dome, referring to its great hemispherical dome.

questions.q: The Gol Gumbaz is associated with which broad style of architecture?
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz is a prime example of Indo-Islamic architecture in the Deccani style of the Adil Shahi sultanate.

questions.q: How many towers or turrets stand at the corners of the Gol Gumbaz?
questions.explanation: The cube-shaped tomb has four octagonal multi-storey towers, one at each corner.

questions.q: To which Deccan sultanate dynasty did the builder of the Gol Gumbaz belong?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Adil Shah belonged to the Adil Shahi dynasty, which ruled the Sultanate of Bijapur.

questions.q: The Gol Gumbaz was the capital monument of which Deccan sultanate?
questions.explanation: Bijapur was the seat of the Adil Shahi sultanate, and the Gol Gumbaz is its grandest monument.

questions.q: The internal diameter of the Gol Gumbaz dome is approximately which of the following?
questions.explanation: The dome spans an internal diameter of roughly 38 metres (about 124 feet), making it one of the largest masonry domes in the world.

questions.q: The Gol Gumbaz dome is often described as one of the largest of its kind in the world, second among such single-chamber domes mainly behind which famous structure?
options: The Pantheon in Rome / St. Peter's Basilica
questions.explanation: Its dome is frequently compared to and ranked just after great domes such as the Pantheon and St. Peter's Basilica as one of the largest masonry domes.

questions.q: What architectural device supports the great dome and creates the famous acoustics inside the Gol Gumbaz?
questions.explanation: The dome rests on a system of intersecting pendentive arches that transfer its weight to the walls and shape the whispering gallery's acoustics.

questions.q: Roughly in which year was construction of the Gol Gumbaz completed?
questions.explanation: The mausoleum was completed around 1656, in the mid-17th century.

questions.q: The basic plan of the Gol Gumbaz tomb chamber is in the shape of what?
questions.explanation: The tomb is essentially a large cube—a square-plan hall—surmounted by the hemispherical dome.

questions.q: Which architect is traditionally credited with designing the Gol Gumbaz?
questions.explanation: The Gol Gumbaz is traditionally attributed to the architect Yaqut of Dabul, who served the Adil Shahi court.

questions.q: Which government body is responsible for protecting and maintaining the Gol Gumbaz today?
questions.explanation: The Gol Gumbaz is a centrally protected monument under the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: Besides Mohammed Adil Shah himself, the Gol Gumbaz also contains the graves of which people?
options: Members of his family including wives and relatives
questions.explanation: The tomb's central platform holds the graves of Mohammed Adil Shah along with members of his family, such as his wives and other relatives.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Gol Gumbaz?
options: Dark grey basalt / stone masonry with mortar
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz is built largely of the dark grey-green basalt stone of the Deccan, set with mortar, rather than marble.

questions.q: Which UNESCO recognition status best describes the Gol Gumbaz?
options: A full UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1983
An ASI-protected Monument of National Importance, included on India's UNESCO Tentative List
questions.explanation: Gol Gumbaz is an ASI Monument of National Importance and the monuments of Bijapur feature on India's UNESCO World Heritage Tentative List rather than as an inscribed site.

questions.q: Mohammed Adil Shah, for whom the Gol Gumbaz was built, was which ruler in the line of the Adil Shahi dynasty?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Adil Shah was the seventh sultan of the Adil Shahi dynasty of Bijapur.

questions.q: In the Whispering Gallery of the Gol Gumbaz, sounds are famously said to echo approximately how many times?
questions.explanation: Guides commonly note that a sound in the Whispering Gallery can echo about seven to ten times due to the dome's acoustics.

questions.q: Within the four corner towers of the Gol Gumbaz, how does one typically reach the Whispering Gallery at the base of the dome?
options: By a ramp on the roof
By climbing spiral staircases inside the corner towers
questions.explanation: Each of the four octagonal corner towers contains a staircase, and visitors climb these to reach the gallery encircling the dome's interior.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor was a contemporary adversary against whose empire Mohammed Adil Shah's Bijapur sultanate had to contend in the Deccan?
questions.explanation: Bijapur under Mohammed Adil Shah was pressured by the Mughals under Shah Jahan, and the sultanate was finally annexed by Aurangzeb in 1686.

questions.q: The Gol Gumbaz complex also includes which associated structure within its grounds?
options: A mosque and a gateway/Naqqar Khana (now a museum)
questions.explanation: The complex includes a mosque and a gateway building, the Naqqar Khana, which now houses an archaeological museum.

questions.q: The unsupported single chamber beneath the Gol Gumbaz dome is celebrated as one of the largest of its kind. What makes this especially notable?
options: It uses no walls at all
It is one of the largest single chambers covered by a dome without intermediate supporting pillars
questions.explanation: The hall is famed as one of the world's largest single chambers spanned by a dome with no internal supporting pillars.

questions.q: The dome of the Gol Gumbaz rests on a circular drum that springs from the walls; the transition from the square hall to the circular dome base is achieved chiefly by what?
questions.explanation: The square-to-circle transition is managed by squinch-like intersecting pendentive arches, a hallmark of Deccani dome construction.

questions.q: The Adil Shahi dynasty that built the Gol Gumbaz traced its origins to a founder of what background?
options: Yusuf Adil Shah, of Persian/Turkic origin who founded Bijapur's independence from the Bahmanis
questions.explanation: The Adil Shahi dynasty was founded by Yusuf Adil Shah, of Persian/Turkic origin, who established Bijapur's independence as the Bahmani Sultanate fragmented.
